practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the use of various measuring instruments (e.g., rules, vernier calipers, and micrometer screw gauges) to determine length.
Demonstrate the use of clocks and devices for measuring an interval of time.
Investigate factors that affect the period of a simple pendulum.
Determine the density of irregularly shaped objects using the displacement method.
Investigate the relationship between mass and acceleration using trolleys and ticker tape timers.
Investigate the effect of force on a spring (Hooke's Law).
Demonstrate the effects of friction on the motion of a body.
Investigate the turning effect of a force (Principle of Moments).
Measure pressure using a mercury barometer and manometers.

subtopics: 11.3.3 Expansion of solids, liquids and gases
11.3.5 Heat transfer by conduction, convection and radiation
practical_and_experimental_content: Calibrate and use thermometers.
Experiment with the thermal expansion of solids, liquids, and gases.
Observe and demonstrate heat transfer in fluids (convection currents).
Experiment to distinguish between good and bad absorbers/emitters of radiant heat (e.g., shiny vs. dull surfaces).
Measure the specific heat capacity of solids and liquids experimentally (e.g., using the electrical method or method of mixtures).

subtopics: 11.4.1 Simple ideas of the wave motion theory
practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the propagation of waves using ripple tanks, springs, and ropes.
Investigate the properties of sound using tuning forks and sonometers.
Determine the speed of sound in air experimentally.
Investigate the reflection of light using plane mirrors.
Perform experiments to trace the path of light through a rectangular glass block and a triangular prism.
Investigate the focal length of converging and diverging lenses using optical pins and ray boxes.
Demonstrate the making (magnetisation) and destroying (demagnetisation) of magnets.
Investigate induced magnetism.
Demonstrate the plotting of magnetic field lines using iron filings and plotting compasses.
Demonstrate the existence of static charges using ebonite and polythene rods.
Investigate the detection of electric charges using a gold-leaf electroscope.

subtopics: 12.9.1 Electric charge, current, and potential difference
12.9.4 Heating effect of an electric current
practical_and_experimental_content: Experimentally measure potential difference (P.D.) and current using voltmeters and ammeters.
Demonstrate the charging and discharging of a lead-acid accumulator.
Demonstrate how to determine resistance in a simple circuit (Ohm's Law).
Experiment with the magnetic field patterns of electric currents.
Demonstrate the practical application of electromagnets (e.g., electric bells and relay switches).

subtopics: 12.10.2 The simple A.C. and D.C. generators
practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the phenomenon of electromagnetic induction using coils and magnets.
Investigate the principles of step-up and step-down transformers.
Investigate the properties of cathode rays by using a Cathode Ray Oscilloscope (CRO).
Investigate the nature of radioactivity and demonstrate safety precautions when handling radioactive substances (using appropriate models or Geiger-Muller counters if available).
